page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-192GB is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 shared vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 12.0 GB per core. This configuration does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 11309.02 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 8193.0. Single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance rated in the bottom 10% at 746.4. Memory bandwidth is stable, recording 102.97 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 74.72 GB/sec for large block write operations. Passmark memory performance is relatively weak, scoring 1412.21, while database operations perform at an average level with 7431.86 operations.

The shared CPU allocation model presents a resource-density tradeoff, making this server highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated single-core CPU performance. It is well-suited for large-scale caching, in-memory databases, Redis deployments, and moderate multi-threaded processing tasks such as software compilation or static web serving.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-192GB is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 12.0 GB per core ratio. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows average multi-core performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 8193.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 23491.5, though single-core performance is weak. Memory bandwidth is moderate, reaching 102.97 GB/sec for large block reads. Given the shared CPU allocation and high memory capacity, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads. It is best suited for in-memory databases, Redis caching, and moderate multi-threaded applications.
